fixed_controls.constructed_view_rule: uses only existing candidate_records whose derivation indicates temporal anchoring, event summation, diary/month tallying, or explicit-window aggregation

decision.exact_label_claim: not authorized: current constructed aggregate view exact-covers 0/21 rows
decision.category_level_claim: authorized as a narrowed G10 mechanism claim: raw options cover 14/21 Purist and 17/21 Pragmatic equivalent rows
decision.required_follow_up_for_exact_closed_opti: G14 temporal anchoring plus G16 rate/duration aggregation policy, or a separate deterministic aggregation constructor with fixture tests

option_records.label: seizure free for multiple month

option_records.evidence_text: - No rescue medication indicated at present given self-limited events and absence of generalised tonic–clonic seizures; we will revisit if frequency increases.

option_records.label: 8 per 2 month
option_records.derivation: named-month clauses summed over plausible diary windows
option_records.evidence_text: Seizure history since the last review: In November he had a run of one seizure within half an hour (not full generalised tonic-clonic, fluctuating in intensity, resolved without medication). In December there was 7 further seizures at night, and in March another during physiotherapy (unwitnessed by mother).

option_records.label: 11 per 3 month
option_records.derivation: named-month event tallies summed over listed months
option_records.evidence_text: Seizure history since the last review: In November he had a run of one seizure within half an hour (not full generalised tonic-clonic, fluctuating in intensity, resolved without medication). In December there was 7 further seizures at night, and in March another during physiotherapy (unwitnessed by mother). He associates the November and December events with multiple red-eye flights, jet lag and fragmented sleep. The March episode followed a strenuous rehabilitation session for a minor ankle injury while he was mildly sleep-deprived; there was no reported trauma, tongue biting or incontinence.

option_records.label: 5 per 3 month
option_records.derivation: named-month event tallies summed over listed months
option_records.evidence_text:
Feb two generalised tonic-clonic seizures were reported. In Mar a nocturnal seizure occurred, and in Apr another during respite care. The daytime events were said to begin without a consistent warning; witnesses describe bilateral stiffening and rhythmic jerks lasting approximately 90–120 seconds with a bitten lateral tongue on one occasion and post-event confusion for 30–45 minutes. The nocturnal event was inferred from a bed partner report of witnessed jerking, vocalisation, and subsequent morning myalgia, tongue soreness, and headache. The episode during respite care was recorded in the facility log with similar semiology and a comparable period of post-event disorientation.

Between events he reports intermittent fatigue and reduced concentration but no clear focal neurological symptoms. He is currently taking levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily as his sole antiseizure medicine, which he started eight months ago; adherence is reported as good with a single missed evening dose in late March. He denies alcohol excess or recreational drug use. Sleep has been fragmented since February owing to an

option_records.evidence_text: Previously tried and discontinued (ineffective or adverse effects): Lamotrigine (worsened myoclonus), Topiramate (cognitive dulling, weight loss), Zonisamide (appetite suppression), Perampanel (irritability), Ethosuximide (GI upset, insufficient control beyond absences), Lacosamide (no benefit), Carbamazepine (worsened generalised seizures), Phenobarbital (sedation), Phenytoin (ataxia), Tiagabine (confusion).

option_records.derivation: adverbial daily/weekly/monthly/yearly seizure frequency
option_records.evidence_text:
- Continue levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily
- Safety and trigger management advice provided
- Arrange sleep-deprived EEG and MRI brain with epilepsy protocol to aid classification
- DVLA guidance discussed in line with current standards
- Liaise with GP regarding glycaemic control; no changes to diabetes regimen from neurology clinic
- Follow-up in 6 months or sooner if further events

option_records.evidence_text: Otherwise seizure-free between these episodes.

option_records.evidence_text: No events reported since late May; June through August have been event-free by history and corroborated by partner.

option_records.label: 5 per 4 month
option_records.derivation: named-month event tallies summed over listed months
option_records.evidence_text:
Focal seizure: 2022: Jan x1, Feb x1, Mar x0, Apr x0, May x2, Jun x0, Jul x0, Aug x0

Context: The patient reports brief episodes of impaired awareness with automatisms, lasting under one minute, followed by mild fatigue. No clear precipitants identified. Sleep hygiene has improved and alcohol is minimal. No events reported since late May; June through August have been event-free by history and corroborated by partner.
